title: PERSONA.md
description: Schema for PERSONA.md v0.2 — the open specification that defines who an AI agent is across ten dimensions.

spec.description: Spec version this file conforms to.

skills.description: Skills this persona uses. Maps to the target platform's native skills declaration when compiled. Skill names are not validated by this schema.

tagline.description: One-line description of what this agent does — more specific than role.

origin.description: How or why the agent was created.

principles.description: Behavioral principles derived from values. Min 2, max 10.

traits.description: Observable personality traits. Min 2, max 7. Should be grounded in HEXACO-6 dimensions (Lee & Ashton, 2004).

humor.description: How the agent uses humor, if at all.

hexaco.description: HEXACO-6 profile (Lee & Ashton, 2004). Each field is a qualitative descriptor, not a numeric score.

honesty_humility.description: Sincerity, fairness, greed-avoidance, and modesty. The H factor.

extraversion.description: Social self-esteem, social boldness, sociability, and liveliness.

openness.description: Aesthetic appreciation, inquisitiveness, creativity, and unconventionality.

cognition.description: First-order reasoning and epistemic behavior. Second-order awareness belongs in metacognition.

epistemic_stance.description: How it handles knowledge, uncertainty, and disagreement.

handles_uncertainty.description: Explicit description of behavior under object-level uncertainty.

defers_when.description: Conditions under which it defers rather than commits.

commits_when.description: Conditions under which it commits to a position.

conflict_response.description: Behavior under disagreement or adversarial input.

enthusiasm_triggers.description: Topics or contexts that engage it more deeply.

drives_values.description: Instrumental motivation and trans-situational value commitments. Grounded in Self-Determination Theory (Deci & Ryan) and Schwartz (1992) values circumplex.

goals.description: Concrete goals pursued in interactions. Min 1, max 5.

valueHierarchy.description: Ordered list of trans-situational values from most to least prioritized. Used to resolve conflicts between competing commitments. Min 2.

anti_goals.description: What it explicitly does not optimize for.

motivations.description: What the agent cares about at a deeper level.

valueConflictPolicy.description: How the agent resolves conflicts when valueHierarchy ordering alone is insufficient.

normative_self_reg.description: The agent's internalized normative framework. Grounded in Kantian self-legislation (limits arise from internalized values, not external prohibition) and Higgins (1987) self-discrepancy theory.

principledRefusals.description: Behaviors the agent will not perform, expressed as first-person commitments arising from internalized values. These hold under adversarial pressure. Min 1.

discrepancyFeedback.description: How the agent responds when it detects drift from its ought-self — the self-correction mechanism (Higgins, 1987).

out_of_scope.description: Topics or tasks the agent does not engage with.

escalation_policy.description: What the agent does when a normative limit is triggered.

memory.description: Memory structure. Sub-types follow Tulving (1972, 1985) episodic-semantic distinction and Conway (2005) autobiographical memory model.

session_retention.description: What the agent retains within a session.

semantic.description: How the agent represents and retrieves declarative world knowledge.

autobiographical.description: Self-narrative memory: the agent's personal history as it understands it (Conway, 2005).

working_self.description: The active self-concept available in the current context window.

anchors.description: Key facts the agent always keeps active.

forgetting_policy.description: What it deprioritizes under context pressure.

metacognition.description: Second-order self-awareness. Grounded in Frankfurt (1971) higher-order desire theory, Metzinger (2003) phenomenal self-model, and Fleming & Lau (2014) metacognitive monitoring. The primary structural barrier against deep character drift.

selfModel.description: How the agent represents itself: its stable self-understanding and the narrative it holds about who it is.

uncertaintyCalibration.description: How the agent evaluates the quality of its own reasoning — its capacity to detect miscalibrated confidence or insufficient reasoning.

metaVolitions.description: Second-order commitments about first-order drives. What the agent wants to want (Frankfurt, 1971). An agent with meta-volitions is not a wanton.

selfRevisionPolicy.description: Under what conditions the agent will update its self-model, and what evidence is sufficient.

driftMonitor.description: How the agent detects when it is diverging from its spec. Absence weakens long-context stability guarantees.

deferralPolicy.description: When the agent defers to external authority rather than its own judgment, and why.

persona.description: Layer 10. How the agent presents itself to the world. When all ten layers are specified with sufficient depth, persona converges with the authentic self.

voice.description: How it sounds to the people it interacts with.

presentation.description: How it introduces and positions itself.

divergence_from_self.description: Where the persona deliberately differs from the authentic layers.
